steps:
Step 1: Calculate each sample population.
Sample P: (3/7) × 980 = 3 × (980/7) = 3 × 140 = 420.
Sample Q: 0.45 × 900 = 405.
Sample R: 2³ × 7² + 15 = 8 × 49 + 15 = 392 + 15 = 407.
Sample S: 12.5% of 3200 = 0.125 × 3200 = 400.
Sample T: √(160000) − 2³ = 400 − 8 = 392.
Step 2: List populations in ascending order: 392 (T), 400 (S), 405 (Q), 407 (R), 420 (P).
Step 3: Median is the middle (3rd) value: 405 (Sample Q).
Step 4: Check options: Q corresponds to option B. However, re-check calculations: P=420, Q=405, R=407, S=400, T=392. Sorted: T=392, S=400, Q=405, R=407, P=420. Median is 405 (Q).
